title: 3D COMPRESSIBLE CFD: TRAPEZOIDAL FIN AERODYNAMICS ANALYSIS
org: Advanced Fluid Dynamics, Propulsion, and Energy Lab (AFPEL)
what: Comparative CFD of blunt-edged vs. streamlined trapezoidal fins at Mach 0.7 (Re = 1.58 × 10⁶, AoA = 0°): blunt — flat 90° edges, 5.50/2.144 in chord, AR = 0.720; aero — curved leading + cusped trailing, identical planform
Flow at 236.6 m/s / 283.19 K / 94,212 Pa (2,000 ft ISA); full RANS study from mesh generation through post-processing — pressure/viscous decomposition to isolate wake and boundary layer mechanisms
how: rhoSimpleFoam RANS (Spalart-Allmaras, OpenFOAM v2412); linearUpwind convection, GAMG multigrid; inlet 236.6 m/s / 283.19 K, outlet 94,212 Pa, no-slip adiabatic walls
5.88M hex cells; y⁺ = 2.905 (aero, viscous sublayer, direct skin friction) / y⁺ = 15.757 (blunt, wall functions); max skewness 3.98, avg non-ortho 8.47°
HPC 'Intruder': 192 core-hrs (blunt, 1,013 iter) / 432 core-hrs (aero, 2,498 iter, v-res 6.65×10⁻⁸); ParaView + Python/NumPy/Matplotlib
results: 30.7% drag reduction: Cᴅ 0.02970→0.02057; pressure −29.2% (0.01769→0.01252), viscous −33.1% (0.01201→0.00804); base separation wake = 59.5% of blunt Cᴅ
62.4% lift reduction: Cₗ 0.19837→0.07459, L/D 6.68→3.63 (−45.7%); aero fin viscous Cₗ = −0.10368 from adverse pressure gradient on cusped trailing edge
Blunt fin: constructive pressure + viscous lift (166% more Cₗ); mission framework — streamlined for cruise, blunt for control authority; variable geometry recommended
